coin_profile.name: Aztec
coin_profile.bucket_explanation: A Cash-Flow Protocol is a blockchain or app that earns real money from its users — just like a business. Every time someone makes a trade, borrows money, or uses the network, it collects a small fee. The more people use it, the more fees it earns. We value these tokens similarly to how you would value a business — by looking at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it.
coin_profile.description: Aztec (AZTEC) — a Cash-Flow Protocol ranked #340 by market cap.

AZTEC.name: Aztec

dilution_control.label: Dilution Control
dilution_control.chart_ref: Network Economics → Token Supply: Issuance vs Burns

moat.rationale: Aztec has a narrow moat as a privacy-focused L2 with differentiated technology, but faces significant weaknesses: extreme early-stage maturity (score: 1), almost no dilution control (score: 1), limited token utility with poor value capture for holders (score: 5), and high forking risk as privacy tech is replicable. While network demand scores strongly (10), the protocol lacks switching costs, has no meaningful ecosystem lock-in, and operates in a crowded L2 space where users follow fee economics—similar to ARB/OP but without their adoption advantages. Early revenue and unproven product-market fit make this a pre-moat protocol.

forecast_meta.method_explanation: How we valued this token: We treat this like a small business. The Speculative Fair Value is the average of whichever checks produced a sensible result.
